description_long: This rifle features a robust, angular body with a dark, matte finish, giving it a utilitarian and industrial appearance. The main receiver is blocky, with two cylindrical components extending horizontally on either side, likely representing the handguard or barrel shrouds. Two thin rods protrude vertically from the top, possibly simulating iron sights or mounting rails. The lower section includes a forked or pronged structure, which could represent a stylized magazine or grip. The overall design is symmetrical, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. The surface texture appears slightly rough, suggesting a metal construction with a weathered or worn look.
description: A dark, angular rifle with cylindrical side extensions, vertical rods on top, and a forked lower section, finished in a rough, matte texture.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical, blocky receiver, cylindrical side extensions, and two vertical rods on top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow profile, with the cylindrical extension and vertical rods aligned, and the forked lower section visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, highlighting the symmetry and the same cylindrical and vertical features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the rifle's slim profile and the alignment of its main components.
